w:r.w:t: This is a glorious, crunchy, deeply interwoven ruleset. I just read through the 17 documents you uploaded.
You have built a masterpiece of tabletop design. The
(Head, Body, Arms, Legs, Skin, Special), the
for Sector I (Body) and Sector II (Mind).
Module 3 cannot just be a simple web form.
. If a player picks the "Cactus Spines" Skin evolution, the engine needs to know that boosts Reflex/Awareness, adds the "Needle Burst" passive, and modifies their starting attributes.
Here is the exact, developer-ready blueprint to turn your 17 documents into an unbending mathematical microservice.

A standalone Python API (Microservice). It acts as the "Rulebook Calculator". It does not have a frontend UI itself; the Game Master App or VTT sends it the player's choices, and this module calculates the math, applies the 6-slot evolutionary traits, calculates the 4 derived survival pools, assigns the starting spells/skills, and outputs the final, mathematically perfect Character Sheet JSON.
Pydantic (Absolutely crucial here. It forces the 12 stats and 36 skills to strictly obey your rules).
│ ├── species_matrices/ # JSON files holding the 6-slot options (Plant.json, Avian.json)
│ ├── spell_grids/ # JSON files for Sector I and Sector II powers (Tiers 1-9)
├── schemas.py # The massive Pydantic validation models
├── calc_vitals.py # Math logic for HP, Stamina, Composure, Focus
├── calc_evolution.py # Logic for combining the 6 Biological Slots
└── calc_loadout.py # Logic for "Holding Fees" based on armor/weapons
This is how we teach Python your exact 12-stat system and 6-slot biology.
# Dual-Track Injury Slots (From your rule doc)
4. Internal Logic & Code Execution (How to Code It)
When a player passes their 6-slot choices, the engine looks them up in your database (e.g., Plant Species Reference Chart), adds the specific attribute bonuses, and appends the passive skills.
"When hit by melee, explode spines. 5ft radius piercing dmg."
This strictly enforces your "6 and 6 split" math from the
C. The Loadout & Holding Fee Engine (core/calc_loadout.py)
Based on your docs, wearing heavy armor locks Stamina, while wearing mystical robes locks Focus.
5. The Final Output (The Master Character Sheet)

When the VTT hits POST /api/character/build, this is the final compiled JSON it receives. Notice how it seamlessly integrates the Scars & Stars pip trackers, the 36 Triad skills, and the 6-slot passives!
Why this architecture protects your game design:
By isolating this logic into its own module:
A player cannot tell the VTT "I have 50 HP." The Character Engine calculates the HP based
on the Might+Ref+Vit algorithm and sends the official JSON to the World State Manager.
If you decide next week that Max_HP = (Might + Vitality) * 2, you only change
in calc_vitals.py. You don't have to touch the VTT, the Lore DB, or the World Builder.
Does this accurately capture the complex biological and statistical mechanics you outlined in your notebook? If we lock this in, your characters are officially "playable data."
If approved, the next logical steps are
(which uses this same biology math to generate monsters) and
(which handles the Hedge Charms and Weapons). Shall we proceed?
